title: The Great Simpsina

jokes.text: This activity is so lame I'm gonna daydream about school.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about picking peaches being boring
jokes.punchline: His escape is to fantasize about the thing he normally hates most - school
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - only Bart would hate peach picking so much he'd prefer school. Strong ironic reversal with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Damn it, a pop quiz!
jokes.setup: Previous line about daydreaming about school
jokes.punchline: Even in his fantasy escape, Bart can't catch a break
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-through on the school fantasy joke, showing even Bart's imagination betrays him.

jokes.text: I'm afraid the only 'NPR' we know down here is National Public Radio.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting about authentic folk music like they play on NPR
jokes.punchline: Farmer pretends NPR stands for something rural instead of exactly what it is
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that subverts expectations but relies on familiar urban/rural divide comedy.

jokes.text: Ooh, authentic American folk music, just like they play on NPR!
jokes.setup: Folksy farm worker starts singing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pretentious excitement about 'authentic' folk music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character beat - her intellectual pretension about folk culture

jokes.text: How many verses does this song have? / I ain't never run out!
jokes.setup: Farmer singing increasingly detailed song about individual peaches
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration that the song is literally infinite
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the folk song absurdity with efficient setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: First I work, then I pay, then I have to eat fruit?! Why was I ever born?!
jokes.setup: Homer learning they pay for the peaches they pick themselves
jokes.punchline: His dramatic existential crisis over having to eat healthy food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character comedy - the escalation from work to payment to fruit consumption driving him to question existence itself.

jokes.text: peach-aroni peach-za and my famous BLTs-- or should I say, PPPs?
jokes.setup: Marge listing various peach-based dishes
jokes.punchline: Her proud wordplay turning BLTs into PPPs (peach, peach, peach)
jokes.explanation: Marge's earnest enthusiasm for forced wordplay is character-appropriate but the pun itself is predictable.

jokes.text: That way I can enjoy my massage, and make sure you don't enjoy yours too much.
jokes.setup: Marge booking couples massage
jokes.punchline: Her admission she wants to monitor and limit Homer's pleasure
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in-character insight into Marge's controlling nature disguised as romantic gesture.

jokes.text: I forget what phase two is, but I'll assume we just dump the peaches like we discussed. / I'll assume that means 'yes.' / The name's Bart.
jokes.setup: Boys conducting military-style operation to dump peaches
jokes.punchline: Bart correcting Milhouse's use of 'Roger' with his actual name
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of military pretense with Bart's literal-mindedness about call signs.

jokes.text: Oh, screw it. the half-doctors/half-hookers who solve everything.
jokes.setup: Homer giving up on something (context unclear)
jokes.punchline: Random reference to hybrid doctor-prostitute problem solvers
jokes.explanation: Bizarre and original but lacks clear setup. Typical Homer non-sequitur that's memorable for its strangeness.

jokes.text: What idiot put the dump so far away from where people live?
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart driving to dump peaches
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of understanding of basic urban planning
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer missing the obvious - dumps are located away from people for good reasons

jokes.text: The Horticulture Society is having its Chinese Lantern party and we've got nothing to huck!
jokes.setup: Criminal characters at the dump looking for things to steal
jokes.punchline: Snake's concern about disappointing the refined Horticulture Society
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - criminals caring about high society garden parties creates perfect comic dissonance.

jokes.text: This neighborhood is starting to look a little bit like Sesame Street! Wonder who's in there. Could it be Oscar the...
jokes.setup: Lisa noticing the neighborhood looks like Sesame Street and wondering about Oscar the Grouch
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of actual trash can/Oscar setup that gets interrupted
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference that sets up visual gag but doesn't quite land the payoff.

jokes.text: Help! I'm trapped in an alternate dimension-- an existential nether space! I... Hey, how ya doin'?
jokes.setup: Dramatic cries for help from mysterious magical situation
jokes.punchline: Casual greeting when he realizes someone is there
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from cosmic horror to casual politeness. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: All right, who sent you-- Marvin The Magician? Oswald of the Occult? Citywide Mortgage?
jokes.setup: Suspicious magician listing potential enemies
jokes.punchline: Including his mortgage company among mystical rivals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that grounds magical paranoia in mundane financial reality. Great character-building joke.

jokes.text: Sounds like most of the house is still owned by the bank. But the rest of it is magic.
jokes.setup: Describing house as magical
jokes.punchline: Admitting most of it is actually foreclosed upon
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of magical mystique undercut by economic reality.

jokes.text: I got this phone for subscribing to Psychic Monthly, and then I cancelled the subscription. They never saw it coming.
jokes.setup: Mentioning subscription to psychic magazine
jokes.punchline: The supposed psychics couldn't predict he'd cancel
jokes.explanation: Clean logical joke that exploits the obvious contradiction in psychic services. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Gaze into it and learn how you will die. It's made for the American market.
jokes.setup: Presenting mystical death-predicting mirror
jokes.punchline: Casual mention it's commercially manufactured for Americans
jokes.explanation: Dark magic commercialized for American consumers - good cultural satire with economic timing.

jokes.text: Looks like there was magic between you two.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of Raymondo with his wife
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'magic' meaning both his profession and romantic connection
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun on 'magic' but appropriate for Lisa's character to make wholesome wordplay.

jokes.text: Now I'm alone with my memories and my medical marijuana.
jokes.setup: Sad reflection on being alone with memories
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of medical marijuana to the list
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of melodrama with casual drug reference. Great economy.

jokes.text: Ladies are what you saw in half and throw knives at. They never wear the hat.
jokes.setup: Explaining why women can't be magicians
jokes.punchline: The practical concern that they never get to wear the magician's hat
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Raymondo's old-fashioned sexism through the lens of magic show conventions.

jokes.text: That monkey wound up with 18 car dealerships. If you bought a family car in the St. Louis area, you bought it from the mighty Chimpopo.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Blackstone taught a monkey magic
jokes.punchline: The monkey became a successful car dealer named Chimpopo
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation with specific details that make it funnier. 'Chimpopo' is excellent comic naming.

jokes.text: My ear makes money? Hello, boat store? I'd like to order a boat.
jokes.setup: Seeing Lisa pull coin from his ear
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to buy a boat with the one coin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - seeing magical coin production as a business opportunity for immediate gratification.

jokes.text: In '57, I saw him turn the Secretary of Agriculture into the Secretary of the Interior! It was hell on their wives, but it sure brought down corn prices.
jokes.setup: Grampa remembering Raymondo's magic show
jokes.punchline: Treating cabinet position swap as real with specific consequences
jokes.explanation: Excellent Grampa rambling that treats magic trick as having real political/economic consequences. Great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Built a house outta corn. Worst house I ever owned! When it got real hot, it smelled like Fritos.
jokes.setup: Following up on corn price story
jokes.punchline: Specific sensory detail of corn house smelling like Fritos when hot
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Grampa's corn story with specific, relatable detail that makes the absurd concrete.

jokes.text: I forgot why I'm doing this.
jokes.setup: Bart struggling with magic trick
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his motivation disappearing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's short attention span, but not particularly original.

jokes.text: 'Sup, brahs? It's me, Cregg Demon. Who's ready to get their magic on?!
jokes.setup: Introduction of modern, crude magician character
jokes.punchline: His aggressively casual, bro-ish persona contrasting with traditional magic
jokes.explanation: Establishes character type effectively - the crass modern performer vs. old-school magic dignity.

jokes.text: I'm gonna descend into hell and pull up a skank.
jokes.setup: Cregg describing his magic trick
jokes.punchline: Crude description of summoning a woman from hell
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent crudeness that contrasts with traditional magic, but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Well, I don't need your number, sweetie... because it's tattooed on my ass! Yeah, it's on everyone's ass! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Seeming to do mind-reading trick
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a fake trick because everyone has the same tattoo
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Cregg's tricks are fake, consistent with his character as a hack.

jokes.text: Any idiot with a soul patch and a deck of cards thinks he's a magician.
jokes.setup: Raymondo's disdain for modern magicians
jokes.punchline: Specific physical detail of soul patch marking amateur magicians
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven observation with specific visual detail that rings true about amateur performers.

jokes.text: feed your props.
jokes.setup: Failed rabbit trick
jokes.punchline: Casual reminder about animal care responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that treats dead rabbit as a maintenance issue rather than tragedy.

jokes.text: The only magic we couldn't make was a child.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's passing on magic to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'magic' to describe both his profession and failed fertility
jokes.explanation: Beautifully crafted line that uses magic metaphor for genuine emotion. Excellent economy and character depth.

jokes.text: Now the first thing you must do is make me a vodka tonic. You're out of tonic. That won't be a problem.
jokes.setup: Raymondo asking for specific cocktail ingredients
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he'll drink straight vodka instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Raymondo's drinking problem, but relatively straightforward setup/payoff.

jokes.text: God, if you don't bring my Lisa back safe, ants will burn tonight.
jokes.setup: Homer praying for Lisa's safety during magic trick
jokes.punchline: His threat to torture ants if prayer isn't answered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer character comedy - his prayer bargaining with innocent insects shows perfect twisted logic.

jokes.text: I don't know what to think about milk cans anymore.
jokes.setup: Witnessing Lisa's magic trick
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis about mundane kitchen objects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse-style overreaction to simple magic trick. Great absurdist response with excellent economy.

jokes.text: I can see why they call you The Great Simpsina. / My friends call me Lisa. / She has no friends.
jokes.setup: Martin complimenting Lisa's stage name
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal correction about her social status
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling cruelty from Bart, deflating Lisa's modest response with harsh truth. Excellent timing.

jokes.text: You trick a dorky little girl into telling you! / Yo, Dad! It's a three-quarter flange-less friction slide.
jokes.setup: Cregg revealing how he learned the trick
jokes.punchline: His son providing technical magical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Cregg's family dynamic and technical magic knowledge mixed with crude methods.

jokes.text: Oh, and the next time it's my weekend with you, I promise there will be no chicks over. Just you, me, the komodo dragon and Ben Roethlisberger.
jokes.setup: Cregg promising quality father-son time
jokes.punchline: Bizarre guest list including dangerous reptile and NFL quarterback
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random escalation that reveals Cregg's warped priorities and lifestyle. Specific celebrity reference adds absurdity.

jokes.text: It was Harry H-Dog Houdini, yo! Now, after calling me 'the new him'-- his words--
jokes.setup: Cregg claiming ghost visitation from Houdini
jokes.punchline: Adding 'H-Dog' to Houdini's name and claiming endorsement
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Cregg's need to modernize and claim validation from magic legends.

jokes.text: Lisa, don't feel bad. Judas betrayed Jesus, but he still got paid.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Lisa about her betrayal
jokes.punchline: Focusing on Judas getting money rather than the moral implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer comfort attempt that completely misses the moral point and focuses on profit. Excellent character comedy.

jokes.text: A peanut butter and pretzel sandwich? Mmm! There's no way a stupid Flanders like me could ever have thought of this.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain secrets being given to enemies
jokes.punchline: Ned enjoying the sandwich while self-deprecating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's self-deprecation, though the sandwich metaphor is somewhat forced.

jokes.text: Hey, kid, nobody likes a gloomy magician. Except, of course, Gloomo the Magnificent.
jokes.setup: Telling Lisa not to be gloomy
jokes.punchline: Exception for magician whose name is literally based on being gloomy
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with good structure. Creates fictional magician that makes logical sense within the exception.

jokes.text: I briefly became a disco magician. Have you ever tried to put a leisure suit on a bird? You get his pants on, he pulls his coat off, and on it goes.
jokes.setup: Admitting past shameful career choice
jokes.punchline: Specific practical difficulties of dressing birds in disco clothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd with specific visual details that make the ridiculous scenario concrete and funny.

jokes.text: Wish I could help, Chief. I'll take the box that doesn't talk back. / Yeah, that's just what you need, Chief, another stomach. Shut up.
jokes.setup: Lou choosing magic box over talking to Chief Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Box immediately proves to talk back and insult the Chief
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff to Lou's choice. The box's specific insult about Wiggum's weight adds character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Hope you're watching up there, Houdini-Bro. I did your great-grand-niece.
jokes.setup: Cregg addressing Houdini's spirit before performing
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual boast about Houdini's fictional relative
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent crudeness but relies heavily on shock value. Effective at showing Cregg's tastelessness.

jokes.text: No. With my back, I got to sit.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if he's just going to stand there while someone dies
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about his back problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with mundane age-related concerns. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Lisa, magicians hate to be rescued.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to save drowning magician
jokes.punchline: Professional pride prevents accepting help during tricks
jokes.explanation: Insightful observation about performer psychology during crisis

jokes.text: You ain't saving nobody demon nothing freek!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to save Cregg Demon Magicfreek
jokes.punchline: Penn's confused attempt to use Cregg's made-up words
jokes.explanation: Mild comedy from Penn struggling with Cregg's terminology. Character-appropriate but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: That was the curse the witch put on us. / He stole all our best tricks. And he stole my sexiest magical gestures. Like this one-- ah.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Teller doesn't speak onstage
jokes.punchline: Penn demonstrating stolen 'sexy' magical gesture
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist explanation for Penn & Teller's real act, plus visual comedy of Penn's 'sexy' gesture.

jokes.text: his tattoos are an inconsistent mix of hieroglyphics and cuneiforms
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Cregg despite his flaws
jokes.punchline: Specific academic criticism of his tattoo historical accuracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character comedy - only she would criticize tattoos for historical inaccuracy. Excellent specificity.

jokes.text: Penn, just drop them! / I can't! I never learned how!
jokes.setup: Teller telling Penn to drop something in a fight
jokes.punchline: Penn admitting he only knows how to juggle, not stop
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy playing on Penn's juggling skills becoming a liability. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: The real magic is raising three kids in this economy!
jokes.setup: Homer performing rescue/magic trick
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on economic challenges of parenting
jokes.explanation: Relatable parental humor that fits Homer's character but is somewhat predictable for economic commentary.

jokes.text: I thought I killed you in Marrakesh. / That was my twin brother. / Ruined a lot of my tricks.
jokes.setup: Penn recognizing Raymondo from apparent murder
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of fraternal murder affecting magic career
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comedy with perfect deadpan delivery. The casual concern about trick ruins makes it funnier.

jokes.text: To show you we mean business, I'm unbuttoning my shirt and turning on a fan. Wah. Oh, yeah. It's magical.
jokes.setup: Penn threatening with intimidation
jokes.punchline: His attempt at sexiness with shirt and fan
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that works through absurdity but relies heavily on visual execution for full impact.

jokes.text: Penn, there never was a curse. / You're a real jerk, you know that?
jokes.setup: Teller speaking after 'curse is broken'
jokes.punchline: Using his voice to immediately insult Penn
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the curse explanation - Teller's first words being an insult to his partner is excellent character comedy.

jokes.text: Penn, there never was a curse. You're a real jerk, you know that?
jokes.setup: Teller finally able to speak after 'curse' is broken
jokes.punchline: First thing he says is insulting his partner
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Penn & Teller dynamic - reveals their real relationship after the act

jokes.text: I'm through with magic, son. I'm gonna move back to Canada and run for Parliament.
jokes.setup: Cregg giving up magic after near-death experience
jokes.punchline: His random decision to pursue Canadian politics
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist career change that fits Cregg's random personality. Canadian Parliament is oddly specific choice.

jokes.text: Tell me if she falls.
jokes.setup: Raymondo going to his dressing room during Lisa's performance
jokes.punchline: Casual request to be informed if his student dies
jokes.explanation: Dark teacher humor about student safety

jokes.text: Hello, Harvey. I've probably had enough. Eh, a little more couldn't hurt.
jokes.setup: Barney at the bar during magic show
jokes.punchline: His internal monologue about drinking decisions
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney rationalization of continued drinking

jokes.text: Shh. / You shush yourself.
jokes.setup: During endless peach song in credits
jokes.punchline: Homer and Marge's marital bickering continues even during end credits
jokes.explanation: Bookends the episode with the endless song and typical Homer-Marge dynamic

tmdb_overview: When the Great Raymondo takes Lisa on as his magician's assistant, she is conned into revealing the secret of his greatest trick to his nemesis.
